contraindications: May promote tumor growth in active cancer. Avoid with anticoagulants.
timingRules: Administer on empty stomach. Split dose AM/PM for sustained effect.

contraindications: Contraindicated in active malignancy. Not recommended during pregnancy.
timingRules: Loading phase: 2x/week for 4 weeks. Maintenance: 1x/week.

contraindications: MAOI interaction — severe hypertensive crisis risk. Avoid with cardiovascular disease. Reduces efficacy of oral contraceptives.
timingRules: Take within 1 hour of waking. Do not take after 12 PM to preserve sleep architecture.

contraindications: Caution with anticoagulants (may potentiate bleeding). Avoid in mushroom allergy.
timingRules: Best taken with meals. Morning dose preferred for cognitive benefit.

contraindications: Avoid with acetylcholinesterase inhibitors. Caution in trimethylaminuria (TMAU).
timingRules: Take 30-60 min before cognitive tasks or training. Can split AM/PM.

contraindications: Thyroid-stimulating — avoid in hyperthyroidism. May potentiate sedatives and thyroid medications.
timingRules: Evening dose preferred for sleep. With food to reduce GI discomfort.

contraindications: Renal impairment — risk of hypermagnesemia. Caution with antibiotics (chelation).
timingRules: Split dose: 1 cap AM, 2 caps 1 hour before bed.

contraindications: Avoid in acute psychosis. Not recommended during pregnancy/lactation.
timingRules: Intranasal AM dosing. Effects onset within 5 min, redose every 4-6 hours if needed.

contraindications: Avoid combining with benzodiazepines (potentiation). Pregnancy contraindicated.
timingRules: Intranasal 2-3x daily. Can stack with Semax for synergistic nootropic/anxiolytic effect.

contraindications: Caution with copper overload disorders (Wilson's disease). Avoid in active skin infections.
timingRules: Topical: 2x daily. Injection: 3x/week. Cycle 8 weeks on, 4 weeks off.

contraindications: May slow heart rate — avoid with bradycardia. Can increase GI motility.
timingRules: Take with fat-containing meal for absorption. Effects build over 8-12 weeks.

contraindications: Generally well tolerated. May potentiate antihypertensives.
timingRules: Pair with stimulants to smooth the curve. 200mg AM with caffeine, 400mg PM for sleep.

contraindications: May exacerbate mania in bipolar disorder. Mild MAOI activity — caution with serotonergic drugs.
timingRules: Take AM on empty stomach. Avoid evening dosing (mildly stimulating).

contraindications: Avoid in hypertension (may raise blood pressure acutely). Caution with other stimulants.
timingRules: Sublingual 10-30mg 2x daily. Cycle 56 days on, 4 days off.

contraindications: Renal impairment (renally cleared). May increase bleeding risk — caution with anticoagulants.
timingRules: Split into 2-3 doses daily. Must co-supplement choline source.

contraindications: Fat-soluble — bioavailability depends on dietary fat. Caution in hepatic impairment.
timingRules: Take with fat-containing meal. 2-3x daily due to short half-life.

contraindications: Banned by WADA (athletic competitions). Tolerance develops rapidly — cycle strictly.
timingRules: AM only (stimulating). Max 2-3x/week to prevent tolerance. Cycle 2 weeks on, 2 off.

contraindications: Theoretical concern in active cancer (NAD+ fuels rapid cell division). Monitor blood glucose.
timingRules: AM dosing on empty stomach. Sublingual preferred for bioavailability.

contraindications: Estrogenic activity — caution in hormone-sensitive cancers. May potentiate anticoagulants.
timingRules: Take AM with fat. Pair with NMN for sirtuin activation synergy.

contraindications: Renal impairment (lactic acidosis risk). Depletes B12 long-term. Avoid with excess alcohol.
timingRules: Take with dinner. Extended release preferred. Supplement B12 concurrently.

contraindications: Potent CYP3A4 inhibitor — major drug interactions. Avoid with Metformin (hypoglycemia risk). Pregnancy contraindicated.
timingRules: Take with meals. Split 3x daily (500mg each). Cycle 8 weeks on, 2 off.

contraindications: Anxiety disorders, cardiac arrhythmias, pregnancy. Avoid stacking with other stimulants beyond tolerance.
timingRules: Delay 90 min after waking (adenosine receptor timing). No caffeine after 2 PM.

contraindications: Caution in pre-existing renal disease. Ensure adequate hydration.
timingRules: 5g daily, any time. No loading phase necessary. Consistent daily dosing critical.

contraindications: Paresthesia (tingling) is common but benign. Caution in taurine-deficient individuals.
timingRules: Split into 2-3 doses to minimize paresthesia. Daily loading for 4 weeks.

contraindications: Caution with PDE5 inhibitors (additive hypotension). Avoid with citrullinemia.
timingRules: 6-8g 30-60 min pre-workout. Can take daily for sustained NO benefit.

contraindications: May reduce efficacy of nitroglycerin. Caution in asthma (rare bronchospasm).
timingRules: Take on empty stomach. Separate from minerals by 2 hours (chelation).

contraindications: IV form: monitor for anaphylaxis. Theoretical concern with chemotherapy efficacy.
timingRules: Empty stomach AM. Liposomal form essential for oral bioavailability.

contraindications: Thiamine depletion risk long-term. May cause hypoglycemia with diabetes meds.
timingRules: Empty stomach for best absorption. R-ALA form preferred. Supplement thiamine.

contraindications: Highly experimental — limited human safety data. Possible pro-oncogenic via HGF pathway.
timingRules: Intranasal or topical application. Extremely low doses (1-5mg). Cycle carefully.

contraindications: Experimental compound — no FDA approval. Avoid with SSRIs until more data available.
timingRules: AM dosing. Start at 20mg, titrate to 40mg over 2 weeks.

contraindications: Limited human data. Theoretical tumor promotion via telomerase activation.
timingRules: 10-day cycle, 5-10mg/day SC injection. Repeat cycle every 4-6 months.

contraindications: May worsen depression in some. Caution with immunosuppressants and anticoagulants.
timingRules: 0.3-1mg sublingual 30 min before bed. Higher doses paradoxically worsen sleep.

contraindications: Potent aromatase inhibitor — monitor estrogen levels. Caution in pregnancy.
timingRules: 50mg 1 hour before bed. Can combine with magnesium threonate stack.

contraindications: Generally safe. May interact with clozapine. Very high doses may cause GI upset.
timingRules: 3g 1 hour before bed for sleep. Can also take with collagen AM.

contraindications: Generally safe. Source-dependent allergen risk (marine, bovine).
timingRules: 10-20g daily with vitamin C for hydroxylation. Any time of day.

contraindications: K2 contraindicated with warfarin. Hypercalcemia risk with excess D3. Monitor 25(OH)D levels.
timingRules: Take with fat-containing meal. Morning preferred. Test blood levels quarterly.

contraindications: May increase bleeding risk with anticoagulants. Caution pre-surgery.
timingRules: With meals for absorption. 2-4g EPA+DHA daily. Triglyceride form preferred.

contraindications: May interact with anticoagulants. Gallbladder disease (stimulates bile). Iron chelation at high doses.
timingRules: With fat and black pepper extract (piperine) for absorption. AM/PM split.

contraindications: CYP3A4 inhibition — drug interactions. May potentiate cyclosporine. Caution with fluoroquinolones.
timingRules: Senolytic protocol: Quercetin + Dasatinib, 2 days/month. Daily anti-inflammatory: 500-1000mg.

contraindications: Limited human safety data at high senolytic doses. CYP interaction potential.
timingRules: Senolytic protocol: 20mg/kg for 2 consecutive days monthly. Daily: 100-500mg.

contraindications: Immunosuppressive at higher doses. Lipid elevation. Impaired wound healing. Requires physician oversight.
timingRules: Weekly pulsed dosing (3-6mg). Take on empty stomach. Not daily.

contraindications: SEVERE: Serotonin Syndrome risk with SSRIs/SNRIs/MAOIs. G6PD deficiency — hemolytic crisis. Never combine with serotonergic drugs.
timingRules: Micro-dose: 0.5-1mg/kg oral AM. Turns urine blue (harmless). Pharmaceutical grade only.

contraindications: May reduce warfarin efficacy. Monitor INR if on anticoagulants.
timingRules: Ubiquinol form preferred over ubiquinone. Take with fat. 100-300mg daily.

contraindications: Generally safe. Very high doses may interfere with B vitamins.
timingRules: 10-20mg AM with breakfast. Synergistic with CoQ10.

contraindications: Tolerance develops quickly. Potential for psychological dependence. Avoid daily use.
timingRules: AM dosing only. Max 3x/week. Cycle 2 weeks on, 1 week off.

contraindications: Caution in hormone-sensitive conditions. May lower blood sugar. Avoid with immunosuppressants.
timingRules: AM with breakfast. 200-400mg standardized extract. Cycle 5 days on, 2 off.

contraindications: Testicular toxicity at high doses in animal studies. Cycle strictly. Monitor liver enzymes.
timingRules: AM dosing. Max 600mg/day. Cycle 8 weeks on, 4 weeks off. Monitor bloodwork.

contraindications: Toxicity above 20mg/day. Renal impairment increases risk.
timingRules: 6-10mg daily with food. Consistent daily dosing.

contraindications: Copper depletion with chronic use — supplement copper. Avoid taking with iron simultaneously.
timingRules: Take with food to avoid nausea. 15-30mg daily. Pair with copper (2mg) long-term.

contraindications: Hormone-sensitive cancers. May convert to estrogen or testosterone unpredictably. Monitor via bloodwork.
timingRules: AM dosing mimics natural rhythm. 25-50mg for men, 10-25mg for women. Test DHEA-S levels.

contraindications: Hormone-sensitive conditions. May convert to progesterone, DHEA, or cortisol. Unpredictable downstream effects.
timingRules: AM sublingual for cognitive effects. 10-50mg. Test serum levels regularly.

contraindications: Active cancer. Pituitary tumors. Diabetes (may alter glucose). Not for use under 25.
timingRules: Inject before bed on empty stomach (2hr post-meal). Fasted state critical for GH pulse.

contraindications: Active malignancy. Pituitary disorders. Avoid in pregnancy.
timingRules: Pre-bed injection. Fasted 2+ hours. Combine with Ipamorelin for synergistic GH pulse.

contraindications: Insulin resistance risk with chronic use. Increases appetite significantly. Diabetes contraindicated.
timingRules: Take before bed. Monitor fasting glucose regularly. Cycle 8 weeks on, 4 off.

contraindications: SEVERE: Cancer risk — promotes cell proliferation indiscriminately. Hypoglycemia risk. Requires medical supervision.
timingRules: Post-workout injection. Monitor blood glucose. Short cycles only (4 weeks max).

contraindications: Caution in autoimmune conditions (may exacerbate). Limited data in pregnancy.
timingRules: 1.6mg SC injection 2x/week. Can use daily during acute infection.

contraindications: Uncontrolled hypertension. Nausea is common. Max 8 doses/month. Avoid with cardiovascular disease.
timingRules: SC injection 45 min before activity. Do not exceed 1 dose per 24 hours.

contraindications: Epilepsy (lowers seizure threshold). Renal failure. Allergic reactions to porcine-derived products.
timingRules: IM or slow IV. Clinical protocols: 10-30ml daily for 10-20 day courses.

contraindications: Allergy to animal-derived peptides. Limited Western clinical data.
timingRules: IM injection AM for 10 days. Repeat course every 3-6 months.

contraindications: Medullary thyroid carcinoma history/family. Pancreatitis risk. Gallbladder disease. Not for Type 1 diabetes.
timingRules: Weekly injection. Titrate slowly over 16 weeks. Stay hydrated.

contraindications: Generally very safe. May potentiate sedative medications.
timingRules: 1-3g daily. Can split AM/PM. Pre-workout for performance benefit.

contraindications: Limited BBB penetration orally. Avoid with benzodiazepines and barbiturates. Sedation potentiation.
timingRules: 500-1000mg sublingual before bed. Or 30 min pre-workout for GH pulse.

contraindications: HIGH ADDICTION POTENTIAL. Severe withdrawal (seizures). Never combine with alcohol, benzodiazepines, or other GABAergic drugs. Max 2x/week.
timingRules: Empty stomach AM for anxiolytic effect. Max 2 doses/week. Never consecutive days.

contraindications: May cause GI upset. Theoretical gout risk at very high doses (purine metabolism).
timingRules: Sublingual AM. Part of the Mr. Happy Stack (Uridine + DHA + Choline).

contraindications: Anticoagulant potentiation. Fish allergy (if fish-derived). Algal DHA available.
timingRules: 1-2g daily with meals. Algal form for vegan option.

contraindications: Caution with anticoagulants and immunosuppressants. Mushroom allergy.
timingRules: AM dosing or 1 hour pre-workout. 1-3g daily.

contraindications: Anticoagulant interaction. Hepatotoxicity reports at high doses. Avoid pre-surgery.
timingRules: Evening dosing for sleep benefit. 1-2g dual extract. Cycle monthly.

contraindications: Mushroom allergy. Caution with immunosuppressants.
timingRules: 2-3g daily with meals. Can take consistently long-term.

contraindications: Generally safe. Avoid mega-dosing. Monitor in histamine-sensitive individuals.
timingRules: AM with breakfast. 1-5mg daily. Can combine with intermittent fasting protocol.

contraindications: ADDICTION RISK even at microdoses. Cardiovascular strain. Not for smokers/vapers. Adolescents contraindicated.
timingRules: 1-2mg transdermal patch (cut to size). AM only. Max 3x/week. Never daily.

contraindications: Generally very safe. May potentiate other analgesics.
timingRules: 300-600mg 2x daily. Micronized form for better absorption. Effects build over 2-4 weeks.

contraindications: May potentiate anticoagulants. Rare hepatotoxicity at high doses.
timingRules: Take with meals. AKBA-standardized extract preferred. 300-500mg 2-3x daily.

contraindications: Heavy metal contamination risk in unpurified forms. Avoid in gout (uric acid). Iron overload conditions.
timingRules: AM with warm water. 300-500mg purified resin form. Dissolve fully before consuming.

contraindications: Significant anticoagulant interaction — avoid with blood thinners. Seizure threshold lowering.
timingRules: 120-240mg standardized extract. Split AM/PM. EGb 761 extract preferred.

contraindications: May potentiate anticoagulants. Soy-derived versions: soy allergy caution.
timingRules: 100mg 3x daily or 300mg pre-bed for cortisol. Sunflower-derived preferred.

contraindications: May lower blood pressure — caution with antihypertensives. Caution with hormonal agents.
timingRules: 4-12mg with fat-containing meal. Consistent daily dosing.

contraindications: Oxalate kidney stone risk at very high doses. G6PD deficiency (IV). Hemochromatosis (enhances iron absorption).
timingRules: Liposomal form for high-dose oral. Split doses throughout day. IV: clinical setting only.

contraindications: Hashimoto's thyroiditis (may worsen). Excess iodine: thyroid storm risk. Monitor TSH closely.
timingRules: AM dosing. Start low (150mcg) and titrate. Pair with selenium for thyroid protection.

contraindications: Toxicity above 400mcg/day (selenosis). Narrow therapeutic window.
timingRules: 200mcg daily with food. Essential companion to iodine supplementation.

contraindications: Renal impairment (hypermagnesemia risk). May potentiate muscle relaxants.
timingRules: 200-400mg elemental Mg before bed. Can split dose if GI sensitive.

contraindications: Thyroid suppression at high doses. Kidney function monitoring required. Not interchangeable with prescription lithium carbonate.
timingRules: 5-20mg lithium orotate daily (NOT mg of elemental lithium). AM with food.

contraindications: Avoid with MAOIs (hypertensive crisis — tyramine pathway). Hyperthyroidism. Melanoma (melanin precursor).
timingRules: 500-2000mg AM on empty stomach. 30 min before stressful tasks.

contraindications: NEVER combine with SSRIs/SNRIs/MAOIs — Serotonin Syndrome risk. Avoid with tramadol, triptans.
timingRules: 50-100mg PM for sleep. Always pair with EGCG or green tea to prevent peripheral conversion.

contraindications: Same serotonergic cautions as 5-HTP. Avoid combining with SSRIs/MAOIs. Eosinophilia-myalgia syndrome (historical — contamination).
timingRules: 500-1000mg before bed on empty stomach. Separate from protein meals by 2 hours.

contraindications: May increase TMAO levels (cardiovascular concern). Seizure threshold lowering in susceptible individuals.
timingRules: 500-1500mg AM on empty stomach. Stimulating — avoid PM dosing.

contraindications: Anticoagulant potentiation. May enhance effects of diabetes medications. Avoid pre-surgery.
timingRules: 1-2 tsp cold-pressed oil with meals. TQ (thymoquinone) content determines quality.

contraindications: May have estrogenic effects. Avoid in hormone-sensitive conditions.
timingRules: AM dosing. 100-200mg standardized extract. Can use long-term.

contraindications: Goitrogenic potential — caution in thyroid conditions. Avoid in hormone-sensitive cancers.
timingRules: 1.5-3g gelatinized powder AM with food. Black maca for male fertility, red for female.

contraindications: Avoid with high-dose arginine (antagonistic). Blood pressure effects — monitor.
timingRules: 500-1000mg 2-3x daily. Empty stomach. Separate from protein by 1 hour.

contraindications: Similar cautions to caffeine but lower risk profile. Avoid late in day.
timingRules: 100-200mg AM. Can combine with caffeine at lower doses. No tolerance buildup observed.

contraindications: Limited long-term data. Similar caution profile to caffeine.
timingRules: 100-200mg. Fast onset (15 min). Stack with Theacrine for extended curve.

contraindications: Contains L-DOPA — avoid with MAOIs (hypertensive crisis), Dopaminergic drugs, and psychosis. Melanoma concern (L-DOPA → melanin).
timingRules: AM on empty stomach. 15-30% L-DOPA extract. Cycle 5 days on, 2 off.

contraindications: Avoid in pregnancy at therapeutic doses (uterotonic). Bipolar mania risk. Expensive — adulteration common.
timingRules: 30mg standardized extract daily. AM or split AM/PM.

contraindications: Anticoagulant potentiation. Pregnancy contraindicated (teratogenic). Avoid pre-surgery.
timingRules: 10-20mg 2-3x daily with food. Bioavailability dramatically improves with fat.

contraindications: Renal disease. High doses may cause DNA damage (controversial). Monitor blood sugar if on diabetes meds.
timingRules: 200-1000mcg daily with meals. Take with carb-containing meals for best effect.

contraindications: Estrogenic properties — caution in hormone-sensitive cancers. May interact with CYP substrates.
timingRules: 200-400mg silymarin 2-3x daily. Phytosome form for enhanced absorption.

contraindications: Dependence potential. CNS depression with alcohol/opioids. Renal dosing required. Never combine with other GABAergic compounds.
timingRules: Prescription required. Start low, titrate slowly. Split 2-3x daily.

contraindications: May increase in-group bias. Pregnancy (uterotonic). Nasal congestion reduces efficacy.
timingRules: 20-40 IU intranasal 30 min before social situations. Not for daily use.

contraindications: Thyroid-stimulating — avoid in hyperthyroidism. Nightshade family — solanaceae allergy. May potentiate sedatives.
timingRules: 600mg full-spectrum extract. AM or PM depending on goal. With food.

contraindications: Autoimmune conditions (immune-stimulating). May interfere with immunosuppressants.
timingRules: 5-50mg daily. Very expensive at pure form. AM dosing preferred.
